scope_boundary: Fresh local RankFold CLI replay over one generated public-safe synthetic text fixture only: RAW0 pack/verify/list/unpack roundtrip-exact behavior plus a rejected single-byte archive mutation. NeuralFold was disabled by local license and no compression advantage was observed.
transition_reason: The public-safe replay probe is useful because it records command outcomes, exact roundtrip digest preservation, RAW0 codec observation, license-disabled NeuralFold boundary, and corrupt-archive rejection. It remains a tiny synthetic replay with no compression advantage and no enabled NeuralFold path, so it blocks promotion for compression-ratio, codec-correctness, downstream-utility, fallback-execution, deployed-compression, benchmark, or chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: licensed or otherwise enabled NeuralFold compression path
negative_results: single-byte archive mutation is rejected by rfa verify
RAW0 archive is larger than the tiny synthetic input
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: RAW0 replay described as NeuralFold compression
no-compression-advantage run described as a compression-ratio result
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed RankFold replay or compression claim after an enabled compression path, public-safe corpus, deterministic decoder review, downstream utility/fallback tests, benchmark context, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: one tiny synthetic public-safe text fixture
RAW0 storage rather than NeuralFold compression
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent compression, codec-correctness, benchmark, or downstream-utility audit
acceptance_blockers: no downstream utility or fallback-execution test
non_claims: does not promote RankFold/NeuralFold above argument

does not prove RankFold codec correctness, NeuralFold compression, compression advantage, benchmark performance, downstream utility, fallback execution, deployed compression behavior, model quality, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote the RankFold chapter core claim
